unit_21 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_21

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 498 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.
答案：A　revisionist
approachprevailprevailing
approach: standard unit headword | prevail: standard unit headword | prevailing: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　optimism; fully shared
sharespanlife span
share: standard unit headword | span: standard unit headword | life span: standard unit headword
#3 TC DOUBLE
Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)__________. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)__________ the number of other species there.Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)__________. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)__________ the number of other species there.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　exaggerated; related to
invasivesubtlecontend
invasive: standard unit headword | subtle: standard unit headword | contend: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive
not leastproponent
not least: standard unit headword | proponent: standard unit headword
#5 SE
One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.
答案：D, F　shrieking; caterwauling
croonwarble
croon: standard unit headword | warble: standard unit headword

Observers of modern presidential campaigns who (i)__________ the highly (ii)__________ productions that pass for campaigns these days do sometimes find reason for hope in the occasional mix-ups that (iii)__________ candidates on the trail despite the presence of political strategists plotting every event with the tactical precision of military commanders.Observers of modern presidential campaigns who (i)__________ the highly (ii)__________ productions that pass for campaigns these days do sometimes find reason for hope in the occasional mix-ups that (iii)__________ candidates on the trail despite the presence of political strategists plotting every event with the tactical precision of military commanders.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　despair over; choreographed; rattle
choreographrelish
choreograph: standard unit headword | relish: standard unit headword
#7 SE
The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly __________ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly __________ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.
答案：D, F　capture; secure
perpetuateintensify
perpetuate: standard unit headword | intensify: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
He defended the governments equivocations regarding the mysterious contrail as resulting not from __________ as critics say, but from a benightedness about its own doings that is almost inevitable, given the multitudinousness of its agencies.He defended the governments equivocations regarding the mysterious contrail as resulting not from __________ as critics say, but from a benightedness about its own doings that is almost inevitable, given the multitudinousness of its agencies.
答案：B　mendacity
abnegationdefend
abnegation: standard unit headword | defend: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　fervor; indiscriminate

#10 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
presagequell
presage: standard unit headword | quell: standard unit headword
#11 SE
The ambassador's critical remarks seemed to be less a product of __________ and more the careless utterances of a fatigued or undisciplined individual.The ambassador's critical remarks seemed to be less a product of __________ and more the careless utterances of a fatigued or undisciplined individual.
答案：C, D　purposefulness; design
utteranceintensity
utterance: standard unit headword | intensity: standard unit headword
#12 TC TRIPLE
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
aggregatemask
aggregate: standard unit headword | mask: standard unit headword
#13 TC SINGLE
Some suggest that students who are granted privileges receive them because their teachers wish to make the students __________; more probably, causality flows in the opposite direction, in that teachers are likely to grant privileges to diligent students.Some suggest that students who are granted privileges receive them because their teachers wish to make the students __________; more probably, causality flows in the opposite direction, in that teachers are likely to grant privileges to diligent students.
答案：D　grateful
conscientiouscausality
conscientious: standard unit headword | causality: standard unit headword
#14 TC DOUBLE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
renderspontaneous
render: standard unit headword | spontaneous: standard unit headword

The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i) __________ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows toward the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii) __________ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii) __________ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a transitional; anticipate; predecessor
derivativesupersede
derivative: standard unit headword | supersede: standard unit headword

The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.

Blank 2

Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

affliction: standard unit headword | grumble: standard unit headword

Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　demarcates; apocryphal; senescence
demarcatenegligible
demarcate: standard unit headword | negligible: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　complicated; knotty
interrelated
interrelated: standard unit headword
#21 TC SINGLE
If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting
historic
historic: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　snag; hitch
hitch

#23 TC DOUBLE
Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate
defensive
defensive: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　yield; engender

One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
historicism

答案：C　perfunctory

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 3

altruistic: standard unit headword

To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i) __________ of nation-states must be (ii) __________ to international institutions.To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i) __________ of nation-states must be (ii) __________ to international institutions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　sovereignty; subordinated
insofar as
insofar as: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　scant; limited
dwindle
dwindle: standard unit headword

答案：B　extol

extol: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　infectious; contented

impel: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
gross
gross: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　undermined; impaired

答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous

Blank 2

#62 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
materialize
materialize: standard unit headword
#63 SE
There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is __________ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is __________ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.
答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly

somber: standard unit headword

Holston characterized a colonial situation as an aggregation of activities and a conjunction of outcomes that, though __________ and at times coordinated, were usually diffuse, disorganized, and even contradictory.Holston characterized a colonial situation as an aggregation of activities and a conjunction of outcomes that, though __________ and at times coordinated, were usually diffuse, disorganized, and even contradictory.
答案：D　linked

impute: standard unit headword

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
controvert
controvert: standard unit headword

Blank 2

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic

答案：A　reliability

In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

Blank 2

#99 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event

In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to __________ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to __________ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.
答案：B　obscure
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　disparities; variances

defensive: standard unit headword
#103 TC SINGLE
Since many African farmers face a soil fertility problem, providing funding for fertilizer seems __________; closer examination of the data raises some troubling questions, however.Since many African farmers face a soil fertility problem, providing funding for fertilizer seems __________; closer examination of the data raises some troubling questions, however.
答案：C　unimpeachable
question
question: standard unit headword
#104 TC TRIPLE
Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report
subtle
subtle: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.
答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original
question
question: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
authoritative
authoritative: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)__________ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)__________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)__________ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)__________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
question
question: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.
答案：B　addiction to
approach
approach: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　negligible; revived

答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated

approach: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword
#113 SE
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#114 TC DOUBLE
The school system’s modest plan for curriculum improvement has (i)__________ local educators: some call it (ii)__________ effort, while others say it is a pragmatic approach given the complexity of the task.The school system’s modest plan for curriculum improvement has (i)__________ local educators: some call it (ii)__________ effort, while others say it is a pragmatic approach given the complexity of the task.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　divided; halfhearted
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#115 TC SINGLE
If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting
historic
historic: standard unit headword
#116 TC TRIPLE
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
debunk
debunk: standard unit headword
#117 SE
The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be __________, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be __________, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.
答案：D, F　unfounded; unjustified
share
share: standard unit headword

Up to the 1970s, historians of science tended to be (i)__________, not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii)__________ in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitive on such scores.Up to the 1970s, historians of science tended to be (i)__________, not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii)__________ in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitive on such scores.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　anachronistic; major
not least
not least: standard unit headword
#119 TC TRIPLE
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed
derive
derive: standard unit headword

The era spanning the collapse of the Western Roman Empire up to the tenth century was a rich and creative period in the development of Europe and thus deserves a less __________ designation than the “Dark Ages” label it customarily receives.The era spanning the collapse of the Western Roman Empire up to the tenth century was a rich and creative period in the development of Europe and thus deserves a less __________ designation than the “Dark Ages” label it customarily receives.
答案：C　dismissive

Agencies responsible for protecting natural resources too often issue permits allowing exploitation of those resources, a process that remains __________ given that agencies have become experts at masking their decisions in scientific terms.Agencies responsible for protecting natural resources too often issue permits allowing exploitation of those resources, a process that remains __________ given that agencies have become experts at masking their decisions in scientific terms.
答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

A (i)__________ of this method is that its predictions (ii)__________ actual observations, but the method also makes the questionable assumptions that the future will resemble the present.

This is not (iii) __________ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i) __________ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii) __________ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii) __________ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace

#124 TC SINGLE
It is difficult to fathom why today's intellectual historians adopt such __________ approach in their work, which seems determined to constrict past thinkers within such narrow social contexts that the wider significance of their ideas disappears.It is difficult to fathom why today's intellectual historians adopt such __________ approach in their work, which seems determined to constrict past thinkers within such narrow social contexts that the wider significance of their ideas disappears.
答案：A　a deflationary

答案：A, C　honed; enhanced

Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of

The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii) __________ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

Blank 1
Blank 2

#135 TC TRIPLE
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

答案：D　an adjunct to

#137 SE

答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for

Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii) __________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.

Zealous volunteers (i) __________ the project's organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii) __________ by the (iii) __________ of the volunteers, Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.

答案：D　aplomb

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize

#147 TC TRIPLE
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.
答案：D　universality

答案：A, B　discordance; contention

Blank 2

Because of its extensive root system, the plant defies attempts to (i)__________ it by normal methods, and consequently, park officials have (ii)__________ efforts to do so. Warming temperatures have also (iii)__________ of the thistle's fortune.An invasive creeping weed native to the Mediterranean region, Cirsium arvense has been present in Yellowstone National Park at least since the nineteenth century. Because of its extensive root system, the plant defies attempts to (i)__________ it by normal methods, and consequently, park officials have (ii)__________ efforts to do so. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　control; largely abandoned; helped

#152 SE
For decades, Pluto seemed to be the mysteriously __________ planet: it was first thought to be about as large as Earth, but, subsequently, measurements had it smaller and smaller.For decades, Pluto seemed to be the mysteriously __________ planet: it was first thought to be about as large as Earth, but, subsequently, measurements had it smaller and smaller.
答案：C, E　dwindling; contracting
dwindle

#153 TC SINGLE
The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.
答案：A　verisimilitude
render
render: standard unit headword
#154 TC DOUBLE
Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional
share
share: standard unit headword
#155 TC TRIPLE
Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation
insofar as
insofar as: standard unit headword
#156 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：D, F　cursory; perfunctory
perfunctory
perfunctory: standard unit headword

The system of thought approached a fundamentally __________ domain as though it had a unitary essence, and the system was therefore inevitably in error.The system of thought approached a fundamentally __________ domain as though it had a unitary essence, and the system was therefore inevitably in error.
答案：A　heterogeneous
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#158 TC DOUBLE
Although the vast weight of evidence supports the contention that the products of agricultural biotechnology are environmentally (i) __________, many people still find them (ii) __________ unsettling.Although the vast weight of evidence supports the contention that the products of agricultural biotechnology are environmentally (i) __________, many people still find them (ii) __________ unsettling.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　sound; intrinsically
contention
contention: standard unit headword
#159 TC TRIPLE
Common sense tells me some people are more (i)__________ than others. The claim that these differences are (ii)__________, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)__________ our everyday observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.Common sense tells me some people are more (i)__________ than others. The claim that these differences are (ii)__________, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)__________ our everyday observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　altruistic; illusory; contradicts
altruistic
altruistic: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
In modern times, friendship has become a __________ relationship: a form of connection in terms of which all are understood and against which all are measured.In modern times, friendship has become a __________ relationship: a form of connection in terms of which all are understood and against which all are measured.
答案：C　paradigmatic
contentious
contentious: standard unit headword
#161 SE
The author advocates a diminished role for philosophy, aiming to show that many of the questions traditionally debated among philosophers can be __________ the realm of scientific inquiry.The author advocates a diminished role for philosophy, aiming to show that many of the questions traditionally debated among philosophers can be __________ the realm of scientific inquiry.
答案：A, F　ceded to; left to
question
question: standard unit headword
#162 TC DOUBLE
The description of Green's scholarship as (i)__________ is grossly misleading: while her research on interstellar particles is not especially novel, the conclusions she draws from her data are (ii)__________.The description of Green's scholarship as (i)__________ is grossly misleading: while her research on interstellar particles is not especially novel, the conclusions she draws from her data are (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering
derivative
derivative: standard unit headword
#163 TC TRIPLE
Excitement is often considered anathema in science writing because it interjects the scientist's emotion and values into the work. Indeed, one should not be too quick to (i)__________ of science. At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.Excitement is often considered anathema in science writing because it interjects the scientist's emotion and values into the work. Indeed, one should not be too quick to (i)__________ of science. At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　abandon the objectivity; dispassionate; misleading
stance
stance: standard unit headword

In a production process that is complex and often unpredictable, roles that start out discretely defined may become quite __________.In a production process that is complex and often unpredictable, roles that start out discretely defined may become quite __________.
答案：A　confused
perfunctory
perfunctory: standard unit headword
#165 SE
While a normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of plants, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of highly intensified floods that do not have such welcome effects.While a normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of plants, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of highly intensified floods that do not have such welcome effects.
答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable for
intensify

Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; a staleness

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　divided; halfhearted

答案：A　jarring

答案：A, B　belies; masks

#170 TC DOUBLE
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
question
question: standard unit headword
#171 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
span
span: standard unit headword
#172 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predate that event
debunk
debunk: standard unit headword

答案：E　conjectural

答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

perpetuate: standard unit headword

答案：D　disingenuous

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　partially offset by; shrinking; strengthening

答案：E　implausible

As human interactions become (ii) __________ physical location, people are less likely to have regular dealings with others who do not share the same values and outlooks.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　provincialism; less contingent on

However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.(i) __________, we can safely infer causality by appealing to a set of general principles (Newtonian mechanics, for example) that are well understood and have been shown to apply in comparably straightforward circumstances. However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　In simple situations; complex; unjustified

答案：C　ubiquitous

Blank 2

Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.Those who blame the inadequacies of science education for students' lack of reasoning skills perpetuate their unproductive notion of science as (i) __________, unique in its capacity to inoculate us against superstition and ignorance. Blank 2

Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：E　paradigmatic

However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work's validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

答案：A　plastic

In what seems a self-conscious (ii) __________ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.Argument may be an overly (i) __________ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Hummingbirds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii) __________ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.

Blank 2

Sometimes the criteria that are used to categorize nation-states are purely factual: for example, the denotation of a state as a coastal state or an inland state. But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.Sometimes the criteria that are used to categorize nation-states are purely factual: for example, the denotation of a state as a coastal state or an inland state. But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　evaluative; share; constitute the basis of
share
share: standard unit headword
#203 SE
The scholar defended W.E.B. Du Bois from the charge of being __________ by pointing out that whenever Du Bois changed his views, he acknowledged it, explained his reasons for doing so, and moved on.The scholar defended W.E.B. Du Bois from the charge of being __________ by pointing out that whenever Du Bois changed his views, he acknowledged it, explained his reasons for doing so, and moved on.
答案：A, C　inconsistent; erratic

答案：E　disingenuous

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　a privileged; a disproportionate

adolescent: standard unit headword
#206 SE
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious

But it is harmful insofar as it (iii)__________ the fields' mutual enrichment.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　divergence; understandable; hampers

答案：B　magnanimity

Although the (i)__________ and the not infrequent appearance of terms like synaptogenesis and N-methyl-D-aspartate glutamate receptor can be (ii)__________ in a book aimed at the general reader, Konner's overall style is (iii)__________ enough that you feel as though you are in the company of a helpful and hopeful teacher who is eager to share what he's found.Although the (i)__________ and the not infrequent appearance of terms like synaptogenesis and N-methyl-D-aspartate glutamate receptor can be (ii)__________ in a book aimed at the general reader, Konner's overall style is (iii)__________ enough that you feel as though you are in the company of a helpful and hopeful teacher who is eager to share what he's found.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　repeated use of jargon; daunting; conversational

答案：D　rarer than

Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)__________, as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.Because leadership in the computer hacker group is extremely (i)__________, when law enforcement agencies arrested several prominent members, taking them offline, the group (ii)__________. Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)__________, as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　fluid; did not collapse; fungible

And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.

Blank 2
Blank 3

That definition would not be (i) __________; that is, it does not contradict the evidence. Its (ii) __________, however, renders it less (iii) __________ than definitions based on astronomical criteria, such as orbit, size, and gravitation.The traditional set of nine planets could have been retained by defining a planet as any object that has the unique combination of characteristics of any one of the nine objects that were considered planets in the late twentieth century. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　false; arbitrariness; defensible

Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.Among the rising generation of concert pianists, a (i)__________ approach to performance is sadly (ii)__________. Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.

Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles

This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. derive: standard unit headword
#259 SE
Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.
答案：E, F　magnified; intensified
intensify
intensify: standard unit headword
#260 TC SINGLE
Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.
答案：A　proprietary
shareperpetuate
share: standard unit headword | perpetuate: standard unit headword
#261 TC DOUBLE
Many civic institutions tend to (i) __________, when they do not actively discourage, the better natures of the citizenry. People are individually altruistic social animals who nonetheless teach themselves to be (ii) __________ in the public sphere.Many civic institutions tend to (i) __________, when they do not actively discourage, the better natures of the citizenry. People are individually altruistic social animals who nonetheless teach themselves to be (ii) __________ in the public sphere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　ignore; selfish
altruistic
altruistic: standard unit headword
#262 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) __________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) __________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
defend
defend: standard unit headword
#263 SE
Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.
答案：B, F　supplant; replace
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE
The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so __________ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so __________ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.
答案：D　pervasive
subtlequestion
subtle: standard unit headword | question: standard unit headword
#265 TC DOUBLE
Standard thermal evolution models of giant planets employ initial conditions that are, to some extent, arbitrarily chosen, selected more for computational expediency than for physical accuracy. Since eventually the initial conditions (i) __________ the evolving planet, this approach is more (ii) __________ for mature planets than it is for young planets.Standard thermal evolution models of giant planets employ initial conditions that are, to some extent, arbitrarily chosen, selected more for computational expediency than for physical accuracy. Since eventually the initial conditions (i) __________ the evolving planet, this approach is more (ii) __________ for mature planets than it is for young planets.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　become irrelevant to; valid
approach
approach: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed

答案：B, E　dogmatic; orthodox

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　obsolescence; outdated

答案：E, F　magnified; intensified

#270 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring

question: standard unit headword
#271 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　idiosyncratic; impugn

In western and central Japan, for example, there was considerable (ii) __________, with many inhabitants deriving their ancestry from the Wa and Yamato peoples and at least a third being descendants of Korean immigrants unrelated to those peoples.It is incorrect to think of eighth-century Japan as (i) __________. In western and central Japan, for example, there was considerable (ii) __________, with many inhabitants deriving their ancestry from the Wa and Yamato peoples and at least a third being descendants of Korean immigrants unrelated to those peoples.
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：D, E　truculence; pugnacity

Too often, the peak seems forever out of reach, always disappearing over the next crest. It's a feeling that (ii) __________ physicists, who have spent much of the last century trudging up a seemingly endless slope in search of truly cosmic vistas. It is proving (iii) __________ climb.

The first of these two theories asserts that widespread mountain building cooled the earth as a result of the (ii)__________ mountains and climate. For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)__________: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.There are two opposing theories about mountain formation and climate over the past 40 million years: either the surge of mountain building (i)__________ the global cooling or vice versa. The first of these two theories asserts that widespread mountain building cooled the earth as a result of the (ii)__________ mountains and climate. For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)__________: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　caused; feedback between; self-perpetuating

At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to

Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
contend
contend: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　enthralled by; dazzled by

The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to

Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic

答案：B, D　an exponent; a champion

Blank 2

Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. 答案：D, F　misrepresent; underestimate

Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by

答案：B, D　nebulous; amorphous

Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original

As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.
Blank 1
Blank 2
