In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.Unlike some of his predecessors and competitors, Schaeffer's distinguished position as a foreign-affairs columnist has not made him (i) __________. His column is breezy and conversational. In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　pontifical; enliven; familiar

If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inferable from; challenged

答案：B, C　specious; deceptive

However, evidence of significant contact between the ancient Mediterranean and sub-Saharan Africa has been (ii) __________, rendering these exogenous explanations (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　artificially produced experiences; spurious

#305 TC TRIPLE
There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deploy; fanciful; purveyed

question: standard unit headword
#306 TC DOUBLE
The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on
intensity
intensity: standard unit headword
#307 SE
Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.
答案：B, D　fortuitous; accidental
trade-off
trade-off: standard unit headword
#308 TC TRIPLE
Preservation of historically significant architecture once (i) __________. But as preservation's scope has widened from individual buildings to entire urban districts, it has become a cultural (ii) __________. Much of the debate involves modernist architecture's role in areas preserved as historic districts. Although preservation professionals often (iii) __________ modernist additions to these settings, there is strong public support for keeping new buildings traditional.Preservation of historically significant architecture once (i) __________. But as preservation's scope has widened from individual buildings to entire urban districts, it has become a cultural (ii) __________. Much of the debate involves modernist architecture's role in areas preserved as historic districts. Although preservation professionals often (iii) __________ modernist additions to these settings, there is strong public support for keeping new buildings traditional.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　provoked little controversy; battleground; advocate
historic
historic: standard unit headword
#309 TC DOUBLE
Even the best-known scholars of public policy are sometimes (i) __________ their subject's (ii) __________ as an academic field: the study of public policy is concentrated in no single academic discipline, has no unifying conceptual framework, and employs no unique methods.Even the best-known scholars of public policy are sometimes (i) __________ their subject's (ii) __________ as an academic field: the study of public policy is concentrated in no single academic discipline, has no unifying conceptual framework, and employs no unique methods.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　skeptical of; coherence
skeptical
skeptical: standard unit headword
#310 SE
Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.
答案：B, F　multifarious; protean
span
span: standard unit headword
#311 SE

答案：B, F　elucidation; explication
interrelated
interrelated: standard unit headword

They share a generally (ii) __________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i) __________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii) __________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious

share: standard unit headword
#314 TC TRIPLE
Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule
masksubtle
mask: standard unit headword | subtle: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain

When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.

Called anammox (for anaerobic ammonia oxidation), the microbe was converting into nitrogen gas in the absence of oxygen, a reaction previously assumed to be (ii) __________.

The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)__________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)__________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)__________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)__________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)__________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)__________ by the frogs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　origin; produce; eaten

Blank 2

The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)__________. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern

The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from

It is true that windmills could be built virtually everywhere, whereas water mills (ii)__________. However, water mill’s great capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.

There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)__________.The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)__________. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

#343 TC DOUBLE
It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　fully vested in them; vitiated

答案：A, E　feigned; false

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　presented; defining features of; propose

There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　unfair; malign; blameless

approach: standard unit headword
#353 SE
In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chang __________ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chang __________ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.
答案：B, C　concede; acknowledge
question
question: standard unit headword
#354 TC TRIPLE
Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)__________ in large part by what they (ii)__________: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)__________ of transportation options.Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)__________ in large part by what they (ii)__________: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)__________ of transportation options.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　characterized; lack; dearth
demarcate
demarcate: standard unit headword
#355 TC DOUBLE
While several relationships fell by the wayside under the pressure of conflicting opinion, none was more affecting to the writer than her relationship with her brother. Her letters document a steady (i) __________ of feeling: intense pride in him giving way to more moderate respect, trust (ii) __________ as feeling of betrayal come to dominate.While several relationships fell by the wayside under the pressure of conflicting opinion, none was more affecting to the writer than her relationship with her brother. Her letters document a steady (i) __________ of feeling: intense pride in him giving way to more moderate respect, trust (ii) __________ as feeling of betrayal come to dominate.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　erosion; evaporating
materializeintensify
materialize: standard unit headword | intensify: standard unit headword
#356 SE
Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
negligible
negligible: standard unit headword
#357 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
pernicious
pernicious: standard unit headword
#358 TC DOUBLE
Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　cavalier disregard for; ironic
approachskeptical
approach: standard unit headword | skeptical: standard unit headword
#359 SE
For certain economists, “pure” economic theory, that is, economic theory __________ a specific social structure, is impossible, much like a concept of anatomy that investigates no specific species.For certain economists, “pure” economic theory, that is, economic theory __________ a specific social structure, is impossible, much like a concept of anatomy that investigates no specific species.
答案：B, D　abstracted from; divorced from
derive
derive: standard unit headword
#360 TC TRIPLE
While the Prime Minister’s long-standing reputation for (i)__________ political power may (ii)__________ his recently stated willingness to devolve real power to regional assemblies and local governments, it certainly does not (iii)__________ his doing it.While the Prime Minister’s long-standing reputation for (i)__________ political power may (ii)__________ his recently stated willingness to devolve real power to regional assemblies and local governments, it certainly does not (iii)__________ his doing it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　centralizing; raise doubts about; preclude

render: standard unit headword
#361 TC DOUBLE
With respect to environmental issues, most legal scholarship tends to envision politics as the pursuit of already fixed interests, a view that (i) __________ the ability of political communities to (ii) __________ their values and their interests through the process of democratic deliberation.With respect to environmental issues, most legal scholarship tends to envision politics as the pursuit of already fixed interests, a view that (i) __________ the ability of political communities to (ii) __________ their values and their interests through the process of democratic deliberation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　ignores; transform

mask: standard unit headword | defend: standard unit headword
#362 SE
Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received __________ attention from international communication researchers.Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received __________ attention from international communication researchers.
答案：B, E　scant; limited
dwindle
dwindle: standard unit headword
#363 TC TRIPLE
The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poisons called batrachotoxins. But the (i) __________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii) __________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii) __________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poisons called batrachotoxins. But the (i) __________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii) __________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii) __________ by the frogs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　origin; produce; eaten
neutralize
neutralize: standard unit headword
#364 TC DOUBLE
Although the political science professor’s paper is quite (i)__________ about the government’s problems, suggesting that they are part of a (ii)__________ process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.Although the political science professor’s paper is quite (i)__________ about the government’s problems, suggesting that they are part of a (ii)__________ process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　pessimistic; degenerative

spontaneous: standard unit headword
#365 SE
British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.
答案：D, F　spurious; specious

derivative: standard unit headword
#366 TC TRIPLE
Unambiguous texts can allow their readers to (i)__________ them quickly, but ambiguous texts can have the attractive (ii)__________ of multiple possible interpretations, all of which can be considered equally (iii)__________, and none of which is the single true meaning.Unambiguous texts can allow their readers to (i)__________ them quickly, but ambiguous texts can have the attractive (ii)__________ of multiple possible interpretations, all of which can be considered equally (iii)__________, and none of which is the single true meaning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　comprehend; allure; valid
inveterate
inveterate: standard unit headword
#367 TC DOUBLE
The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
historicism
historicism: standard unit headword
#368 SE
Although people often describe the correct trajectory for a thrown or moving object, their efforts to explain that trajectory in terms of physics can reveal __________ understanding of the forces acting on the object.Although people often describe the correct trajectory for a thrown or moving object, their efforts to explain that trajectory in terms of physics can reveal __________ understanding of the forces acting on the object.
答案：A, D　a naïve; an unsophisticated

subtle: standard unit headword

In medieval Europe, watermills were more (i)__________ than windmills. It is true that windmills could be built virtually anywhere, whereas watermills (ii)__________. However, watermills' greater capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.In medieval Europe, watermills were more (i)__________ than windmills. It is true that windmills could be built virtually anywhere, whereas watermills (ii)__________. However, watermills' greater capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C, F　precise; circumspect

But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.

Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　established; defining features of; propose

derivative: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　impetuous; circumspect

Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is __________: most people eat much more salt than they need.Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is __________: most people eat much more salt than they need.
答案：C, E　unabated; undiminished

Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt

Just as different human groups have different kinds of musical traditions, different groups of whales have different dialects evident in their songs, and it is possible for one group to influence the (i)__________ of another. It has been documented more than once that a group of whales will (ii)__________ its own tunes and adopt the new sounds of an unfamiliar group.Just as different human groups have different kinds of musical traditions, different groups of whales have different dialects evident in their songs, and it is possible for one group to influence the (i)__________ of another. It has been documented more than once that a group of whales will (ii)__________ its own tunes and adopt the new sounds of an unfamiliar group.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　tastes; abandon

答案：C, F　specious; unfounded

Given So's (iii)__________ many of the players in Hong Kong politics, it is surprising that he did not use interviews and other forms of direct research to delve further into the motivations, strategies, and tactics of participants.A minor criticism of the book, which is (i)__________ an understanding of the difficulty of doing direct research in Hong Kong's, is that So relied on secondary sources to tell the story of Hong Kong's political development, with previous histories of the period (ii)__________ his research. Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself

#401 TC TRIPLE
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
share
share: standard unit headword
#402 SE
In his role as a librarian and historian, Ama Bontemps was a great __________ and a preserver of the documents of Black cultural heritage.In his role as a librarian and historian, Ama Bontemps was a great __________ and a preserver of the documents of Black cultural heritage.
答案：A, C　annalist; chronicler
proponent
proponent: standard unit headword
#403 TC DOUBLE
Many of the towns that have voted to keep incinerators in the county's solid waste plan have done so not because they necessarily (i) __________ incinerators, but because they are (ii) __________ to narrow their waste-disposal options.Many of the towns that have voted to keep incinerators in the county's solid waste plan have done so not because they necessarily (i) __________ incinerators, but because they are (ii) __________ to narrow their waste-disposal options.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　favor; loath
question
question: standard unit headword
#404 TC TRIPLE
There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it's (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it's just 80 or so - 3 percent - that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there's even one we'd like to see in greater numbers: Toxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it's (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it's just 80 or so - 3 percent - that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there's even one we'd like to see in greater numbers: Toxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　unfair; malign; blameless
pernicious
pernicious: standard unit headword
#405 SE
Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.
答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
archetypal
archetypal: standard unit headword
#406 TC DOUBLE
He was never (i) __________; he was nothing if not (ii) __________, so he forbore for the present to declare his passion.He was never (i) __________; he was nothing if not (ii) __________, so he forbore for the present to declare his passion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　impetuous; circumspect
spontaneous
spontaneous: standard unit headword

The enthusiasm that many English artists and writers felt regarding the Labour Party (i)__________ amid complaints that arts funding remained as (ii)__________. And even though the government recently announced a significant funding boost for the Arts Council, some art leaders still refuse to (iii)__________ Labour.The enthusiasm that many English artists and writers felt regarding the Labour Party (i)__________ amid complaints that arts funding remained as (ii)__________. And even though the government recently announced a significant funding boost for the Arts Council, some art leaders still refuse to (iii)__________ Labour.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　waned; parsimonious; trust
intensify
intensify: standard unit headword
#408 SE
The author's point is not that people should be __________ science; he is careful to point out that what science is discovering about humans, for example, is certainly important and worthy of careful consideration.The author's point is not that people should be __________ science; he is careful to point out that what science is discovering about humans, for example, is certainly important and worthy of careful consideration.
答案：B, E　dismissive of; scornful of
skeptical
skeptical: standard unit headword
#409 TC DOUBLE
Although movie critic Pauline Kael had a distaste for sycophancy, she also had a need for (i) __________; as a consequence of these competing feelings, she sent very (ii) __________ signals to friends and colleagues.Although movie critic Pauline Kael had a distaste for sycophancy, she also had a need for (i) __________; as a consequence of these competing feelings, she sent very (ii) __________ signals to friends and colleagues.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　obeisance; mixed

subtle: standard unit headword
#410 TC TRIPLE
Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional
contentious
contentious: standard unit headword
#411 SE
Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.
答案：A, F　complexity; intricacy
intensity
intensity: standard unit headword
#412 TC DOUBLE
Although the political science professor's paper is quite (i) __________ about the government's problem, suggesting that they are part of (ii) __________ a process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.Although the political science professor's paper is quite (i) __________ about the government's problem, suggesting that they are part of (ii) __________ a process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　pessimistic; degenerative

spontaneous: standard unit headword

There are, it seems, (iii)__________ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)__________ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)__________ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)__________ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　subvert; predictable; a limited
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　tantamount to; commensurate with

#415 TC DOUBLE
The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
historicism
historicism: standard unit headword
#416 TC TRIPLE
For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　accessible; proliferation of detail; oversimplifying
extol
extol: standard unit headword
#417 SE
Coastal waters of oceans are being starved of oxygen at __________ rate, with vast stretches of water along the seafloor depleted of oxygen to the point that they can barely sustain marine life.Coastal waters of oceans are being starved of oxygen at __________ rate, with vast stretches of water along the seafloor depleted of oxygen to the point that they can barely sustain marine life.
答案：B, E　an alarming; a disturbing

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2

If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) __________ challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) __________ papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) __________ throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) __________ challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) __________ papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) __________ throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　colossal; meager; indefatigably

#423 SE
In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.
答案：B, E　static; unvarying
authoritarian

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　emergent; half-formulated

#425 TC TRIPLE
Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added
intensify
intensify: standard unit headword
#426 SE
Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social
altruistic

Just as different human groups have different kinds of musical traditions, different groups of whales have different dialects evident in their songs, and it is possible for one group to influence the (i) __________ of another. It has been documented more than once that a group of whales will (ii) __________ its own tunes and adopt the new songs of an unfamiliar group.Just as different human groups have different kinds of musical traditions, different groups of whales have different dialects evident in their songs, and it is possible for one group to influence the (i) __________ of another. It has been documented more than once that a group of whales will (ii) __________ its own tunes and adopt the new songs of an unfamiliar group.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　tastes; abandon
perpetuate

The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　self-serving; self-regard; self-aggrandizement
abnegation

#429 SE

答案：B, E　oppositionality; protest

Blank 2

In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.

答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon

答案：B, E　functional; utilitarian

At this scale, however, physical (i) __________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at macroscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii) __________.

Blank 2

Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, F　offset; counterbalance

These were edited into books, and the cities' (i) __________ meant that new views were (ii) __________ for successive editions.Among the most popular Currier & Ives lithographic prints in nineteenth-century America were bird's-eye views of great cities such as New York, Chicago, and San Francisco. These were edited into books, and the cities' (i) __________ meant that new views were (ii) __________ for successive editions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　extraordinary growth; regularly required

In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.Ultimately, the young idealists (i) __________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten

答案：C, D　an unfolding; a living

#445 TC DOUBLE
In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i) __________ truth can be highly (ii) __________, as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i) __________ truth can be highly (ii) __________, as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　provisional; useful

#446 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#447 SE
Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.
答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful
negligible
negligible: standard unit headword
#448 TC DOUBLE
Around the early 1980s, technology companies began creating the first portable machines that resemble today's laptop computers. Still, for many years, large and bulky desktop computers (i) __________ the market, because you always had to (ii) __________ something for portability. Compared with laptops, desktops had more power, bigger screens, more comfortable keyboards, and more storage space.Around the early 1980s, technology companies began creating the first portable machines that resemble today's laptop computers. Still, for many years, large and bulky desktop computers (i) __________ the market, because you always had to (ii) __________ something for portability. Compared with laptops, desktops had more power, bigger screens, more comfortable keyboards, and more storage space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　held sway in; sacrifice
share
share: standard unit headword
#449 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude
presage
presage: standard unit headword
#450 SE
Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.
答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　perfectly harmonious; overdrawn

derivative: standard unit headword
#452 TC TRIPLE

The plan says that "parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry." It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii) __________ in parks where it can be performed without (iii) __________ other park values.For scientists who wish to conduct research in the national parks, the new plan promises a warm welcome in place of the (i) __________, or even resistance, that often greets requests for research permits. The plan says that "parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry." It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii) __________ in parks where it can be performed without (iii) __________ other park values.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　indifference; facilitated; jeopardizing

答案：D, E　a prodigious; an immense

答案：B, D　fastidious; finicky

Blank 2

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored

答案：A, F　eschew; avoid

defend: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2

The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.

Blank 2

答案：B, F　abates; dissipates

materialize: standard unit headword
#463 TC DOUBLE
An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　unfettered originality; solipsistic

altruistic: standard unit headword

Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to

But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist

#476 TC TRIPLE
For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical

Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.

Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.The bankruptcy expert has long argued that the entrepreneurial spirit in the United States is (i) __________ the practice that we, as a society, have had of treating risk takers (ii) __________ when they fail. deceptive: standard unit headword | derive: standard unit headword
#494 TC DOUBLE
Proponents of international regulation of environmental issues have always struggled against scientific uncertainty and economic hostility, two obstacles which, from a political standpoint, often have been closely related, as economic hostility toward environmental regulation for economic reasons has (i) __________ the considerable uncertainty underlying most environmental challenges to (ii) __________ of environmental regulation.Proponents of international regulation of environmental issues have always struggled against scientific uncertainty and economic hostility, two obstacles which, from a political standpoint, often have been closely related, as economic hostility toward environmental regulation for economic reasons has (i) __________ the considerable uncertainty underlying most environmental challenges to (ii) __________ of environmental regulation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　exploited; downplay the legitimacy

#495 TC DOUBLE
The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　rationalize; monstrous

#496 TC DOUBLE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　dismiss; faithful

#498 TC DOUBLE
As the semiconductor industry has (i) __________ Moore's law, which predicts that the number of transistors crammed into a chip will double every two years, the architecture of modern computer chips has become increasingly (ii) __________: the holes and trenches that conduct electricity now span only a few nanometers.As the semiconductor industry has (i) __________ Moore's law, which predicts that the number of transistors crammed into a chip will double every two years, the architecture of modern computer chips has become increasingly (ii) __________: the holes and trenches that conduct electricity now span only a few nanometers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　kept pace with; intricate
